Some microorganisms thrive in conditions that would instantly destroy any other life — boiling hydrothermal vents, acidic hot springs, frozen Antarctic ice, and even the vacuum of space. These extremophiles redefine the boundaries of biology. Extremophiles: Life at the Limits

Your body hosts over 38 trillion microbial cells — outnumbering your own cells. This vast community of bacteria, fungi, and viruses forms a complex ecosystem that influences your immune system, mood, and even behavior. The Human Microbiome: A Universe Within
